item.title: FlowInOne:Unifying Multimodal Generation as Image-in, Image-out Flow Matching
item.description: Multimodal generation has long been dominated by text-driven pipelines where language dictates vision but cannot reason or create within it. We challenge this paradigm by asking whether all modalities, including textual descriptions, spa...

item.title: Q-Zoom: Query-Aware Adaptive Perception for Efficient Multimodal Large Language Models
item.description: MLLMs require high-resolution visual inputs for fine-grained tasks like document understanding and dense scene perception. However, current global resolution scaling paradigms indiscriminately flood the quadratic self-attention mechanism...

item.title: SEVerA: Verified Synthesis of Self-Evolving Agents
item.description: Recent advances have shown the effectiveness of self-evolving LLM agents on tasks such as program repair and scientific discovery. In this paradigm, a planner LLM synthesizes an agent program that invokes parametric models, including LLM...

item.title: FP4 Explore, BF16 Train: Diffusion Reinforcement Learning via Efficient Rollout Scaling
item.description: Reinforcement-Learning-based post-training has recently emerged as a promising paradigm for aligning text-to-image diffusion models with human preferences. In recent studies, increasing the rollout group size yields pronounced performanc...

item.title: Personalized RewardBench: Evaluating Reward Models with Human Aligned Personalization
item.description: Pluralistic alignment has emerged as a critical frontier in the development of Large Language Models (LLMs), with reward models (RMs) serving as a central mechanism for capturing diverse human values. While benchmarks for general respons...

item.title: AgentGL: Towards Agentic Graph Learning with LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly rely on agentic capabilities-iterative retrieval, tool use, and decision-making-to overcome the limits of static, parametric knowledge. Yet existing agentic frameworks treat external information...

item.title: Qualixar OS: A Universal Operating System for AI Agent Orchestration
item.description: We present Qualixar OS, the first application-layer operating system for universal AI agent orchestration. Unlike kernel-level approaches (AIOS) or single-framework tools (AutoGen, CrewAI), Qualixar OS provides a complete runtime for het...

item.title: Think in Strokes, Not Pixels: Process-Driven Image Generation via Interleaved Reasoning
item.description: Humans paint images incrementally: they plan a global layout, sketch a coarse draft, inspect, and refine details, and most importantly, each step is grounded in the evolving visual states. However, can unified multimodal models trained o...

item.title: RAGEN-2: Reasoning Collapse in Agentic RL
item.description: RL training of multi-turn LLM agents is inherently unstable, and reasoning quality directly determines task performance. Entropy is widely used to track reasoning stability. However, entropy only measures diversity within the same input,...

item.title: MARS: Enabling Autoregressive Models Multi-Token Generation
item.description: Autoregressive (AR) language models generate text one token at a time, even when consecutive tokens are highly predictable given earlier context. We introduce MARS (Mask AutoRegreSsion), a lightweight fine-tuning method that teaches an i...

item.title: INSPATIO-WORLD: A Real-Time 4D World Simulator via Spatiotemporal Autoregressive Modeling
item.description: Building world models with spatial consistency and real-time interactivity remains a fundamental challenge in computer vision. Current video generation paradigms often struggle with a lack of spatial persistence and insufficient visual r...

item.title: DeonticBench: A Benchmark for Reasoning over Rules
item.description: Reasoning with complex, context-specific rules remains challenging for large language models (LLMs). In legal and policy settings, this manifests as deontic reasoning: reasoning about obligations, permissions, and prohibitions under expl...

item.title: A Systematic Study of Cross-Modal Typographic Attacks on Audio-Visual Reasoning
item.description: As audio-visual multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly deployed in safety-critical applications, understanding their vulnerabilities is crucial. To this end, we introduce Multi-Modal Typography, a systematic study exa...

item.title: The Depth Ceiling: On the Limits of Large Language Models in Discovering Latent Planning
item.description: The viability of chain-of-thought (CoT) monitoring hinges on models being unable to reason effectively in their latent representations. Yet little is known about the limits of such latent reasoning in LLMs. We test these limits by studyi...

item.title: On the Step Length Confounding in LLM Reasoning Data Selection
item.description: Large reasoning models have recently demonstrated strong performance on complex tasks that require long chain-of-thought reasoning, through supervised fine-tuning on large-scale and high-quality datasets. To construct such datasets, exis...

item.title: VenusBench-Mobile: A Challenging and User-Centric Benchmark for Mobile GUI Agents with Capability Diagnostics
item.description: Existing online benchmarks for mobile GUI agents remain largely app-centric and task-homogeneous, failing to reflect the diversity and instability of real-world mobile usage. To this end, we introduce VenusBench-Mobile, a challenging onl...

item.title: Combee: Scaling Prompt Learning for Self-Improving Language Model Agents
item.description: Recent advances in prompt learning allow large language model agents to acquire task-relevant knowledge from inference-time context without parameter changes. For example, existing methods (like ACE or GEPA) can learn system prompts to i...

item.title: Neural Computers
item.description: We propose a new frontier: Neural Computers (NCs) -- an emerging machine form that unifies computation, memory, and I/O in a learned runtime state. Unlike conventional computers, which execute explicit programs, agents, which act over ex...

item.title: Graph-Based Chain-of-Thought Pruning for Reducing Redundant Reflections in Reasoning LLMs
item.description: Extending CoT through RL has been widely used to enhance the reasoning capabilities of LLMs. However, due to the sparsity of reward signals, it can also induce undesirable thinking patterns such as overthinking, i.e., generating redundan...

item.title: Beyond Hard Negatives: The Importance of Score Distribution in Knowledge Distillation for Dense Retrieval
item.description: Transferring knowledge from a cross-encoder teacher via Knowledge Distillation (KD) has become a standard paradigm for training retrieval models. While existing studies have largely focused on mining hard negatives to improve discriminat...

item.title: MoRight: Motion Control Done Right
item.description: Generating motion-controlled videos--where user-specified actions drive physically plausible scene dynamics under freely chosen viewpoints--demands two capabilities: (1) disentangled motion control, allowing users to separately control t...

item.title: GenLCA: 3D Diffusion for Full-Body Avatars from In-the-Wild Videos
item.description: We present GenLCA, a diffusion-based generative model for generating and editing photorealistic full-body avatars from text and image inputs. The generated avatars are faithful to the inputs, while supporting high-fidelity facial and ful...

item.title: R3PM-Net: Real-time, Robust, Real-world Point Matching Network
item.description: Accurate Point Cloud Registration (PCR) is an important task in 3D data processing, involving the estimation of a rigid transformation between two point clouds. While deep-learning methods have addressed key limitations of traditional no...

item.title: TC-AE: Unlocking Token Capacity for Deep Compression Autoencoders
item.description: We propose TC-AE, a ViT-based architecture for deep compression autoencoders. Existing methods commonly increase the channel number of latent representations to maintain reconstruction quality under high compression ratios. However, this...

item.title: Improving Semantic Proximity in Information Retrieval through Cross-Lingual Alignment
item.description: With the increasing accessibility and utilization of multilingual documents, Cross-Lingual Information Retrieval (CLIR) has emerged as an important research area. Conventionally, CLIR tasks have been conducted under settings where the la...

item.title: A Frame is Worth One Token: Efficient Generative World Modeling with Delta Tokens
item.description: Anticipating diverse future states is a central challenge in video world modeling. Discriminative world models produce a deterministic prediction that implicitly averages over possible futures, while existing generative world models rema...

item.title: Learning to Hint for Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is widely used for reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, but it often suffers from advantage collapse: when all rollouts in a group receive the same reward, the group yields zero relati...

item.title: Tunable Soft Equivariance with Guarantees
item.description: Equivariance is a fundamental property in computer vision models, yet strict equivariance is rarely satisfied in real-world data, which can limit a model's performance. Controlling the degree of equivariance is therefore desirable. We pr...

item.title: Fast Spatial Memory with Elastic Test-Time Training
item.description: Large Chunk Test-Time Training (LaCT) has shown strong performance on long-context 3D reconstruction, but its fully plastic inference-time updates remain vulnerable to catastrophic forgetting and overfitting. As a result, LaCT is typical...
